menuCarousel.title: The carrier you build on.
menuCarousel.description: Voice Calling that runs your existing Twilio voice application without rewriting it. Wholesale origination and termination with line-item rates. Messaging with no platform tax. Twenty-seven years moving carrier traffic, one phone number for support.

menuCarousel.title: VoiceML
menuCarousel.description: Run your Twilio app on VoiceTel without rewriting code. Same <Say>, <Gather>, <Dial>, and <Record>. Same webhook signatures. Up to 30% less on list rates.

menuCarousel.title: Voice
menuCarousel.description: STIR/SHAKEN attestation. Global colocation footprint and cloud regions. US-based NOC. Bring your own STT, TTS, and recording storage — pay providers directly, no VoiceTel markup.

menuCarousel.title: Messaging
menuCarousel.tagline: SMS and MMS at wholesale rates
menuCarousel.description: A2P 10DLC for high-volume application messaging. Inbound included. Webhook delivery to your endpoint. No per-message platform tax.

menuCarousel.title: Numbers
menuCarousel.description: Wholesale rates on every number type. Per-number e911 provisioning. CNAM and caller-ID services available. Port-in supported.

menuCarousel.title: SDN appliance
menuCarousel.description: Place a Raspberry Pi in your LAN; it tunnels to VoiceTel core. Solves NAT, ALG, UDP timer, and firewall misconfiguration without changing your routing policies.

menuCarousel.title: Phone apps
menuCarousel.description: VoIP calling on web (no install), Windows, macOS, Linux, iOS, and Android. WebRTC media. One app, three form factors.

menuCarousel.title: Pricing
menuCarousel.description: Every line item published. No 'contact sales' gates. STT, TTS, and recording storage paid directly to your chosen provider with zero VoiceTel markup.

menuCarousel.title: Documentation
menuCarousel.description: VoiceML reference, REST API, integration guides for major PBX platforms and firewall vendors, and a five-minute migrate-from-Twilio playbook.

_note: site.formEndpoints.signup is the AJAX endpoint on portalUrl that the /signup/ form posts to. Site navigation links to /signup/ (the marketing page), not directly to formEndpoints.signup.
